_note: This file is the source for the Terminal in-app documentation panel. It is derived from mod/agi/README.md. Whenever README.md is updated, this file MUST also be updated to keep the in-app help in sync.

sections.name: Core
sections.desc: Built-in functions always available without requirelib()

functions.name: sendResp
functions.desc: Set the HTTP response body.

functions.name: sendJSONResp
functions.desc: Set Content-Type to application/json and write a JSON response.

functions.name: echo
functions.desc: Append text to the current HTTP_RESP.

functions.name: sendOK
functions.desc: Set response to the string "ok".

functions.name: requirelib
functions.desc: Load an AGI library into the current VM. Returns true on success.

functions.name: includes
functions.desc: Load and execute another script relative to the current script's directory.

functions.name: delay
functions.desc: Sleep for the given number of milliseconds. After websocket.upgrade(), also pumps incoming WebSocket messages.

functions.name: exit
functions.desc: Stop script execution immediately.

functions.name: execd
functions.desc: Execute another AGI script asynchronously as a detached process.

functions.name: console.log
functions.desc: Write a line to the server log. In Terminal sessions, output is captured and shown in yellow.

functions.name: newDBTableIfNotExists
functions.desc: Create a database table if it does not already exist.

functions.name: DBTableExists
functions.desc: Return true if the table exists.

functions.name: writeDBItem
functions.desc: Write a string value to the given key.

functions.name: readDBItem
functions.desc: Read a string value from the given key.

functions.name: listDBTable
functions.desc: Return all key-value pairs in the table as an object.

functions.name: deleteDBItem
functions.desc: Delete a single key from the table.

functions.name: dropDBTable
functions.desc: Delete the entire table.

functions.name: pathCanWrite
functions.desc: Return true if the current user can write to the virtual path.

functions.name: getUserPermissionGroup
functions.desc: Return a JSON string describing the current user's permission group.

functions.name: userIsAdmin
functions.desc: Return true if the current user is an administrator.

functions.name: userExists
functions.desc: (Admin only) Return true if the username exists.

functions.name: createUser
functions.desc: (Admin only) Create a new user account.

functions.name: removeUser
functions.desc: (Admin only) Delete a user account.

sections.name: filelib
sections.desc: Virtual filesystem read, write, and metadata operations.

functions.name: filelib.writeFile
functions.desc: Write text content to a virtual path. Creates the file if it does not exist.

functions.name: filelib.readFile
functions.desc: Read text content from a virtual path.

functions.name: filelib.deleteFile
functions.desc: Delete a file at the virtual path.

functions.name: filelib.walk
functions.desc: Recursively list entries. mode: "all", "file", or "folder".

functions.name: filelib.glob
functions.desc: Glob match files. sortMode: "default" or user-defined sort. Does not support ** patterns.

functions.name: filelib.aglob
functions.desc: Advanced glob supporting ** recursive patterns. Cannot scan bare root dirs.

functions.name: filelib.readdir
functions.desc: List directory entries. Returns array of {Filename, Filepath, Ext, Filesize, Modtime, IsDir}.

functions.name: filelib.filesize
functions.desc: Return file size in bytes.

functions.name: filelib.fileExists
functions.desc: Return true if the path exists as a file.

functions.name: filelib.isDir
functions.desc: Return true if the path is a directory.

functions.name: filelib.mkdir
functions.desc: Create a directory (and parents) at the virtual path.

functions.name: filelib.md5
functions.desc: Return the MD5 hash string of a file.

functions.name: filelib.mtime
functions.desc: Return file modification time. parseToUnix=true returns a Unix timestamp; otherwise a formatted string.

functions.name: filelib.rootName
functions.desc: Return the display name of the storage root that owns this path.

sections.name: imagelib
sections.desc: Image dimension, resize, crop, and EXIF operations.

functions.name: imagelib.getImageDimension
functions.desc: Return [width, height] of the image.

functions.name: imagelib.resizeImage
functions.desc: Resize an image and save to dest.

functions.name: imagelib.resizeImageBase64
functions.desc: Resize an image and return it as a base64 data URL. format: "jpeg", "png".

functions.name: imagelib.cropImage
functions.sig: imagelib.cropImage(src, dest, x, y, width, height)
functions.desc: Crop a region from an image and save to dest.

functions.name: imagelib.loadThumbString
functions.desc: Return the cached thumbnail as a base64 string.

functions.name: imagelib.hasExif
functions.desc: Return true if the image has EXIF metadata.

functions.name: imagelib.getExif
functions.desc: Return EXIF data as a JSON string.

functions.name: http.request
functions.desc: Curl-like request. options: {url, method, headers, body, json, form, bodyBase64, contentType, username, password, timeout, followRedirect, responseType}. Returns a response object {ok, status, statusText, headers, body, error}. Body precedence: bodyBase64 > form > json > body; responseType 'base64' returns a binary body base64 encoded.

functions.name: http.get
functions.desc: Perform an HTTP GET and return the response body as a string. headers is optional.

functions.name: http.post
functions.desc: Perform an HTTP POST and return the response body. Without headers/contentType the body is sent as JSON (backward compatible).

functions.name: http.put / http.patch / http.delete
functions.desc: Method helpers built on http.request; each returns the response object. http.delete(url, headers) sends no body.

functions.name: http.postForm / http.postJSON
functions.desc: POST helpers built on http.request. postForm sends application/x-www-form-urlencoded; postJSON sends a JSON body. Both return the response object.

functions.name: http.head
functions.desc: Fetch response headers. Without headerKey returns all headers as JSON; with headerKey returns just that header.

functions.name: http.getCode
functions.desc: Return the HTTP status code for the URL.

functions.name: http.download
functions.desc: Download a URL into the destination virtual directory.

functions.name: http.getb64
functions.desc: Fetch a URL and return the raw bytes as a base64 string.

functions.name: http.redirect
functions.desc: Redirect the client. Default statusCode is 307.

sections.name: share
sections.desc: Create and manage public file share links.

functions.name: share.shareFile
functions.desc: Create a public share link. timeoutSec=0 means no expiry. Returns the share UUID.

functions.name: share.removeShare
functions.desc: Remove an existing share link.

functions.name: share.checkShareExists
functions.desc: Return true if the share UUID is still valid.

functions.name: share.fileIsShared
functions.desc: Return true if the file already has an active share link.

functions.name: share.getFileShareUUID
functions.desc: Return the share UUID for the given file, or false if not shared.

sections.name: appdata
sections.desc: Read-only access to web-root application data files.

functions.name: appdata.readFile
functions.desc: Read a file relative to ./web/. Returns false on error.

functions.name: appdata.listDir
functions.desc: List files in a directory relative to ./web/. Returns an array of relative paths.

functions.name: appdata.getModuleList
functions.desc: Return an array of registered module objects.

functions.name: sysinfo.getCPUUsage
functions.desc: Return CPU usage as a percentage (0–100).

functions.name: sysinfo.getRAMUsage
functions.desc: Return {used, total, percent} memory statistics.

functions.name: sysinfo.getNetworkUsage
functions.desc: Return {rxRate, txRate, rxTotal, txTotal} in bytes/bytes-per-second.

functions.name: sysinfo.getDiskInfo
functions.desc: Return an array of logical disk info objects.

sections.name: ziplib
sections.desc: Archive creation and extraction (zip, tar, tar.gz, gz).

functions.name: ziplib.extractZipFile
functions.desc: Extract a ZIP archive into destDir.

functions.name: ziplib.createZipFile
functions.desc: Create a ZIP archive from one or more source paths.

functions.name: ziplib.extractAnyFile
functions.desc: Auto-detect archive format and extract into destDir.

functions.name: ziplib.createAnyZipFile
functions.desc: Create an archive in any supported format. format: "zip", "tar", "tar.gz", "gz".

functions.name: ziplib.isValidZipFile
functions.desc: Return true if the file is a recognisable archive.

functions.name: ziplib.listZipFileContents
functions.desc: Return a JSON tree string of the archive's contents.

functions.name: ziplib.getFileFromZip
functions.desc: Extract one file to tmp:/ and return its virtual path.

functions.name: ziplib.extract7zFile
functions.desc: Extract all files from a 7z archive into destDir.

functions.name: ziplib.list7zFileDir
functions.desc: List immediate children of a directory inside a 7z archive. Directories are returned with a trailing /.

functions.name: ziplib.list7zFileContents
functions.desc: Return the full contents of a 7z archive as a JSON tree string (same schema as listZipFileContents).

functions.name: ziplib.getFileFrom7z
functions.desc: Extract a single file from a 7z archive to tmp:/ and return its virtual path.

functions.name: ziplib.extractPartial7z
functions.desc: Extract selected files/folders from a 7z archive. paths is a JS array or JSON array string. Folder paths strip their parent prefix; file paths are placed flat in destDir.

functions.name: ziplib.get7zFileInfo
functions.desc: Return metadata about a 7z archive: { fileCount, dirCount, totalUncompressedSize, totalCompressedSize }. totalCompressedSize is always 0 (solid compression).

sections.name: sqlite
sections.desc: SQLite database access for scripts. Not available on linux/mipsle or windows/arm/386.

functions.name: sqlite.open
functions.desc: Open or create a SQLite database at the virtual path. Returns a connection object with exec, query, queryRow, tables, schema, and close methods. Throws SQLiteError on failure.

functions.name: db.exec
functions.desc: Execute a non-SELECT statement. params is an optional JS array of bound values. Returns { lastInsertId, rowsAffected }.

functions.name: db.query
functions.desc: Execute a SELECT and return all matching rows as an array of plain objects.
functions.example:
var rows = db.query("SELECT * FROM t WHERE id > ?", [0]);
rows.forEach(function(r) { console.log(r.id, r.name); });

functions.name: db.queryRow
functions.desc: Like db.query() but returns only the first row object, or null if no rows matched.
functions.example:
var row = db.queryRow("SELECT * FROM t WHERE id = ?", [1]);
if (row) sendJSONResp(row);

functions.name: db.tables
functions.desc: Return the names of all user-created tables in the database.

functions.name: db.schema
functions.desc: Return column metadata for a table as an array of { cid, name, type, notnull, dflt_value, pk } objects (from PRAGMA table_info).

functions.name: db.close
functions.desc: Close the database connection and release the handle.

sections.name: llm
sections.desc: Call any OpenAI-compatible or Anthropic LLM endpoint. Endpoint, API key, default model, pricing, and usage quota are configured in System Settings > AI Integration > AI Model.

functions.name: llm.chat
functions.desc: Send a single-turn text prompt and return the assistant reply string. options is optional (see llm options).

functions.name: llm.chatWithFile
functions.desc: Like llm.chat() but attaches virtual-path files. Images become vision parts; text files are inlined. files may be a single vpath string or an array.

functions.name: llm.request
functions.desc: Low-level call with a full OpenAI-style messages array. Returns the raw response object including choices and usage.

functions.name: llm.usage
functions.desc: Return accumulated token/cost metrics: { totalTokens, totalCost, totalRequests, perModel, currency, ... }.

functions.name: llm.models
functions.desc: Return the configured default model and list of models with pricing entries: { default, models }.

functions.name: llm.listModels
functions.desc: Query the live endpoint for available models (no tokens consumed). Returns { models: [...] }.

functions.name: llm.fileParts
functions.desc: Convert virtual-path file(s) into OpenAI-style content parts for use in llm.request(). Images → image_url data URIs; text files → text parts.

sections.name: cnn
sections.desc: Run image/face vision inference (classification, detection, segmentation, pose, oriented detection, face analysis) against an external CXNNAIO server. Endpoint, token and timeout are configured in System Settings > AI Integration > CNN Inference.

functions.name: cnn.classify
functions.desc: Classify an image (default model mobilenet-v2). file is a virtual path. Returns the server's image.classification envelope.

functions.name: cnn.detect
functions.desc: Run object detection (default model yolo11n). Returns the server's image.detection envelope; set options.render to also get an annotated PNG in rendered_image.

functions.name: cnn.segment
functions.desc: Run instance segmentation (yolo11n-seg). Each item carries a per-instance, box-cropped base64 PNG mask.

functions.name: cnn.pose
functions.desc: Run pose estimation (yolo11n-pose), 17 COCO keypoints per detected person.

functions.name: cnn.oriented
functions.desc: Run oriented/rotated-box detection (yolo11n-obb), intended for aerial/top-down imagery.

functions.name: cnn.faceDetect
functions.desc: Detect faces (default model ultraface-rfb-320). Returns the server's face.detection envelope.

functions.name: cnn.faceLandmarks
functions.desc: Detect 98-point facial landmarks (pfld). Set options.cropped to treat the whole input as one face crop instead of detecting faces first.

functions.name: cnn.faceEmbedding
functions.desc: Return an L2-normalized 128-d embedding vector per face (mbv2facenet).

functions.name: cnn.faceAttributes
functions.desc: Return gender attributes per face (gender-mbv2-0.35). Calls the server's /v1/faces/gender route; response object is face.gender.

functions.name: cnn.faceCompare
functions.desc: Compare two face photos/crops and return their cosine similarity. options: { model, threshold, a_cropped, b_cropped }. No async variant.

functions.name: cnn.analyze
functions.desc: Run several tasks over one image in a single round trip. tasks is an array (classify/detect/segment/pose/oriented/faces/landmarks/attributes); options carries top-level render/async plus per-task blocks keyed by task name.

functions.name: cnn.job
functions.desc: Poll an async job submitted with options.async = true. Returns { id, object, status, created, result, error }; status is queued, running, succeeded or failed.

functions.name: cnn.models
functions.desc: Return the live model registry from the configured server: { object, data: [{ id, object, task, classes, input }, ...] }.

functions.name: cnn.health
functions.desc: Return live server health: { status, version, models_loaded, sessions, uptime_s }.

sections.name: office
sections.desc: ArozOS Office suite document converters (.pptx, .xlsx and .docx import/export). Backed by mod/office.

functions.name: office.pptxToPresentation
functions.desc: Parse a .pptx file into the Slides document body schema (see Office/common/CONTRACT.md). Returns the body as a JSON string; embedded pictures are inlined as data: URLs. Throws on failure.

functions.name: office.presentationToPptx
functions.desc: Build a .pptx from a serialized Slides body JSON string and write it to destVpath. Image objects must be data: URLs; chart objects should carry a client-rendered PNG in props.png. Video/audio are not embedded: each renders as its poster frame and the media files are packed into a sidecar zip next to the pptx. Returns true, or the sidecar zip vpath (string) when one was written.

functions.name: office.xlsxToWorkbook
functions.desc: Parse a .xlsx file into the Sheets document body schema. Returns the body as a JSON string. Values, formulas, styles, number formats, merges and frozen panes are converted; charts are skipped. Legacy binary .xls is rejected.

functions.name: office.workbookToXlsx
functions.desc: Build a .xlsx from a serialized Sheets body JSON string and write it to destVpath. Formulas are written natively so Excel recalculates them. Returns true on success.

functions.name: office.docxToDocument
functions.desc: Parse a .docx file into the Docs document body schema. Returns the body as a JSON string. Paragraphs, headings, inline formatting, links, lists, tables, images, header/footer and page setup are converted. Legacy binary .doc is rejected.

functions.name: office.documentToDocx
functions.desc: Build a .docx from a serialized Docs body JSON string and write it to destVpath. Images must be data: URLs. Returns true on success.

functions.name: office.packToFile
functions.desc: Write an Office suite native file (.doca/.xlsa/.ppta) as a zip container: document.json plus deduplicated binary assets. Media data URLs and legacy media?file= links inside the envelope become embedded assets, making the file portable. Returns true on success.

functions.name: office.unpackFromFile
functions.desc: Read a native Office suite file and return its envelope JSON string with embedded assets re-inlined as data URLs. Legacy plain-JSON documents pass through unchanged.

functions.name: office.unpackToWorkdir
functions.desc: Read a native Office suite container and return its envelope JSON with binary assets extracted into <workdirBase>/<doc-hash>/ and referenced by media?file= links (keeps the JSON small even for video-heavy documents). Legacy plain-JSON files pass through unchanged.

functions.name: office.odtToDocument
functions.desc: Read an OpenDocument Text (.odt) file and return the Docs body schema as a JSON string (formatting, links, lists, tables, pictures as data URLs, page geometry, header/footer, page breaks).

functions.name: office.documentToOdt
functions.desc: Build an OpenDocument Text (.odt) file from a serialized Docs body JSON and write it to destVpath. Same subset as the docx exporter.

functions.name: office.odsToWorkbook
functions.desc: Read an OpenDocument Spreadsheet (.ods) and return the Sheets body schema as a JSON string. ODF of:= formulas are translated back to plain A1 references; styles, widths, merges and cell notes are kept.

functions.name: office.workbookToOds
functions.desc: Build an OpenDocument Spreadsheet (.ods) from a serialized Sheets body JSON and write it to destVpath (formulas rewritten to ODF of:= syntax).

functions.name: office.odpToPresentation
functions.desc: Read an OpenDocument Presentation (.odp) and return the Slides body schema as a JSON string, scaled into the 960x540 editor space (text, images, shapes, lines, tables, backgrounds, notes).

functions.name: office.presentationToOdp
functions.desc: Build an OpenDocument Presentation (.odp) from a serialized Slides body JSON and write it to destVpath. Charts use their client-side PNG raster; video/audio objects are skipped.

functions.name: office.documentToPdf
functions.desc: Build a PDF with real selectable text from a serialized Docs body JSON and write it to destVpath. Renders headings, inline styles, links, lists, tables, inline images, page breaks, page geometry and header/footer with page numbers. Latin-1 core fonts; other characters are transliterated.

functions.name: office.workbookPrintToPdf
functions.desc: Build a real-text PDF from a Sheets print model ({sheets:[{name,colW,rowH,rows:[[{t,b,i,u,fc,bg,al}]]}]}) - formatted display strings plus styles computed by the web client. One A4-landscape section per sheet, columns scaled to fit the page.

functions.name: office.presentationToPdf
functions.desc: Build a real-text PDF from a serialized Slides body JSON: one page per slide at the deck canvas size. Text and tables stay selectable; images/charts embed from data URLs; video/audio render their captured poster frame (props.png) or a generic placeholder.

sections.name: websocket
sections.desc: Upgrade the HTTP connection to a persistent WebSocket session.

functions.name: websocket.upgrade
functions.desc: Upgrade to WebSocket. Also overrides delay() with a message-pumping version. Returns false on failure.

functions.name: websocket.send
functions.desc: Send a text frame to the client. Returns false if the connection is closed.

functions.name: websocket.read
functions.desc: Read next message. Returns string on message, null on timeout (still open), false if closed. Omit timeoutMs to block.

functions.name: websocket.available
functions.desc: Return count of buffered unread messages. Non-blocking.

functions.name: websocket.isClosed
functions.desc: Return true when the connection is no longer active.

functions.name: websocket.onMessage
functions.desc: Assign a callback fired inside delay(). msg = { data, timestamp, type }. Set to null to stop and leave messages in buffer.

functions.name: websocket.close
functions.desc: Send a normal-closure frame and close the connection.

sections.name: scheduler
sections.desc: Register and manage recurring background tasks for a webapp.

functions.name: scheduler.hasPermission
functions.desc: Return true if the current user is allowed to create scheduled tasks.

functions.name: scheduler.registered
functions.desc: Return true if the task is already registered for this user+app.

functions.name: scheduler.register
functions.desc: Register a new background task. scriptName defaults to "cron.agi".

functions.name: scheduler.unregister
functions.desc: Remove a registered task.

sections.name: ffmpeg
sections.desc: Media conversion via ffmpeg. Only available when ffmpeg is installed on the host.

functions.name: ffmpeg.convert
functions.desc: Generic media conversion.

functions.name: ffmpeg.videoConvert
functions.desc: Convert video to the given resolution. progressFile is a virtual path for JSON progress updates.

functions.name: ffmpeg.audioConvert
functions.desc: Convert audio to the given sample rate.

functions.name: ffmpeg.imageConvert
functions.desc: Convert/resize an image. scaleFactor 0.5 = 50% size.

sections.name: notification
sections.desc: Raise notifications to ArozOS users through the core notification system. The delivery channel (Telegram, desktop, email, custom webhook) is chosen by each receiving user's own notification preferences; the script only sets the priority (low / medium / high). Available when the host wired the notification system in.

functions.name: notification.send
functions.desc: Send a notification to the current user. priority is optional and one of "low", "medium" (default) or "high". Returns true on success.

functions.name: notification.sendToUser
functions.desc: Send a notification to another user. Requires admin permission. priority is optional (low / medium / high). Returns true on success.
functions.example:
requirelib("notification");
notification.sendToUser("bob", "Hi Bob", "A message for you", "low");

functions.name: notification.PRIORITY_LOW / PRIORITY_MEDIUM / PRIORITY_HIGH
functions.desc: Convenience string constants ("low", "medium", "high") for the priority argument.
